name: Bittersweet Chocolate Coated Truffles
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons Peanut oil or Almond oil or Walnut oil
recipeInstructions:
For Truffles:
1. Melt the broken chocolate in a double boiler, stirring constantly over low heat. Alternatively, microwave for 2 1/2 to 3 minutes on medium.
2. Remove from heat and stir in butter.
3. Add cream and liqueur. Combine with chopped nuts or candied fruit if desired.
4. Chill for 10-15 minutes, stirring frequently until thick enough to hold a shape.
5. Drop by heaping teaspoon (or #70 scoop) onto foil-lined baking pan. Shape round, if desired.
6. Cover and freeze for 20-30 minutes to set truffles firm.
For Coating:
1. Melt the chocolate as directed in basic truffle recipe. Remove from heat and stir in oil.
2. Cool chocolate to 85-90°F (29-32°C) for dipping.
3. Dip cold, firm truffle into melted chocolate, holding with a fork.
4. Place each truffle onto foil-lined baking pan. Decorate top with nuts or candied fruit, etc.
5. Chill at least 2 hours to set chocolate.
Coats 12 truffles.
